commands.review_summary:
The PR changes trusted tool policy execution and contract tests so unreadable registrations, evaluator failures, malformed decisions, unreadable decision fields, and unreadable adjusted params block tool calls deterministically.

PR surface: Source +228, Tests +216. Total +444 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main directly reads and evaluates trusted policies without these guards, and the PR adds focused contract cases that encode the previously unguarded failure modes; I did not execute tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Protected maintainer PR; the remaining action is human acceptance of the compatibility-sensitive fail-closed behavior and final broad validation, not an automated repair.

target.title: fix(plugins): fail closed on trusted policy errors
target.body:
## Summary

- Fails closed when plugin-owned trusted tool policy registration, evaluator, decision, or adjusted params are unreadable, throwing, or malformed.
- Keeps healthy trusted-policy chaining behavior intact for normal approvals, blocks, and adjusted params.
- Intentionally scoped to runtime trusted-policy execution. Broader fuzz/registration hardening remains out of scope for this PR.

## Linked context

Related #87141

Requested by maintainer as a pruned critical-hardening slice from the larger fuzzing branch.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: malformed or throwing trusted tool policy data can no longer fail open or silently skip the policy gate.
- Real environment tested: final rebased local focused Vitest wrapper; AWS Crabbox changed gate on the pushed patch surface.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/plugins/trusted-tool-policy.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts`
  - `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
  - private-name/private-path branch scrub
  - `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
  - `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`
- Evidence after fix: focused contract test passed 59 tests on final rebased head `c64a7c7fc917`; oxfmt passed; diff check passed; branch scrub found no private paths or private reported names; autoreview was clean.
- Observed result after fix: thrown evaluators, unreadable registrations, malformed truthy and falsey primitive decisions, unreadable decision fields, and unreadable adjusted params all return deterministic blocking results instead of allowing the tool call to continue.
- What was not tested: a live packaged third-party plugin invoking this path.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: AWS Crabbox `run_b94d93376eff` on lease `cbx_ae3cc22f0c5b` ran `pnpm check:changed` but failed in current-main core typecheck at untouched `src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts:844` and `:891`; the same failure appeared on the separate runtime-schema branch gate, so this PR's broad changed gate is blocked by main drift.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/plugins/trusted-tool-policy.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- private-name/private-path branch scrub
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` (blocked by unrelated current-main type errors above)

Regression coverage added:

- trusted policy evaluator throws
- unreadable trusted policy registration
- unreadable policy decision object
- malformed truthy primitive decision
- malformed falsey primitive decisions: `false`, `null`, `0`, and empty string
- unreadable adjusted params

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Malformed trusted policy plugin data now blocks tool calls instead of failing open.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. This tightens tool execution policy handling at the trusted-policy boundary.

What is the highest-risk area?

A malformed trusted policy that previously no-oped now blocks tool execution with a deterministic reason.

How is that risk mitigated?

The behavior is limited to unreadable, throwing, or malformed plugin-owned policy data. Normal allow/block/approval/params chaining is covered by the existing contract tests plus the new fail-closed cases.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Draft PR for maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

GitHub CI, maintainer review, and broad changed-gate rerun after the current-main auth-profile typecheck drift is fixed.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

ClawSweeper/local review found the malformed falsey-decision fail-open gap; this PR now blocks that case and includes regression coverage.
target.author: vincentkoc
